Politics.headline: Trump says Iran's supreme leader is dead after U.S.-Israeli strikes
Politics.detail: Following U.S. and Israeli strikes, former U.S. President Donald Trump has announced the death of Iran's supreme leader.

Politics.headline: Carney backs U.S. action against Iranian nuclear program
Politics.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney has voiced Canada's support for the U.S. mission to dismantle Iran's nuclear program amidst escalating tensions.

Politics.headline: RAF jets flying defensive missions after US-Israeli attack on Iran, Starmer says
Politics.detail: Keir Starmer reports that RAF jets are undertaking defensive missions following the joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran.

Politics.headline: Green Party wins UK by-election in blow to Labour, PM Starmer
Politics.detail: In a significant setback for Labour and Prime Minister Starmer, the Green Party has won a closely-watched UK by-election in a former Labour stronghold.

Politics.headline: Rubio plans Israel trip as Trump says he's 'not happy' with US-Iran talks
Politics.detail: Marco Rubio is planning a trip to Israel, coinciding with Donald Trump expressing his dissatisfaction with the ongoing US-Iran talks.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks sink and oil prices rise as worries about AI, inflation and possible war hit Wall Street
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks sank as Wall Street kept punishing companies that could become losers in the AI revolution, while rising geopolitical tensions and inflation data added to investor concerns, causing oil prices to climb.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump tells Iranians the 'hour of your freedom is at hand' as US-Israel launch strikes on Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: Following major strikes by the U.S. and Israel, Donald Trump addressed the Iranian people, suggesting a significant shift in the region's political landscape.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran moves to choke off Strait of Hormuz traffic, threatening global oil supply: report
Finance & Economy.detail: Amidst rising tensions, Iran is reportedly restricting navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping lane for global oil, potentially impacting the world's oil supply.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks slump as investors fret over inflation data, AI
Finance & Economy.detail: Stocks fell in afternoon trading after a report showed higher-than-expected inflation and as Wall Street continues to fret over AI-related disruption.

Finance & Economy.headline: Ecuador hikes tariffs on Colombian imports to 50 percent starting March 1
Finance & Economy.detail: Ecuador has increased tariffs on Colombian imports to 50 percent, effective today, following an initial 30 percent tariff imposed earlier in February.

Technology.headline: Samsung Agrees to Be More Upfront About the Data Its TVs Collect About You
Technology.detail: Samsung has agreed to be more transparent about the data its smart TVs collect from users, addressing concerns about privacy and data usage. The company will provide clearer disclosures and options for users to manage their data collection settings.

Technology.headline: Block Cuts 40% of its Workforce for AI-Backed Restructuring
Technology.detail: Block, the financial services company led by Jack Dorsey, is reducing its workforce by 40% as it embraces AI to reshape its operations, with the company's shares rising 20% following the announcement. This move demonstrates how AI is impacting employment in the fintech industry.

Technology.headline: Data in the Wild: 40% of Employee AI Use Involves Sensitive Info
Technology.detail: A new study reveals that 40% of employees are using generative AI applications in ways that involve sensitive corporate data, often bypassing official controls and increasing the risk of data breaches. This trend highlights the growing need for organizations to implement robust AI security measures.

Technology.headline: OpenAI strikes Pentagon deal with 'safeguards' as Trump dumps Anthropic
Technology.detail: OpenAI has entered into an agreement with the Pentagon that includes specific safeguards, while Donald Trump has reportedly distanced himself from Anthropic. The deal signifies the increasing involvement of AI companies in defense-related projects.

Technology.headline: Microsoft to auto-launch Copilot in Edge whenever you click a link from Outlook
Technology.detail: Microsoft is set to automatically launch its Copilot AI assistant in the Edge browser whenever a user clicks a link from Outlook, further integrating the AI tool into its productivity suite. This move continues Microsoft's strategy of embedding Copilot in various applications.

Science.headline: Scientists Engineer Bacteria to Eat Cancer Tumors from the Inside Out
Science.detail: Researchers are engineering bacteria to invade tumors and consume them from the inside, offering a novel approach to cancer treatment.

Science.headline: Scientists Observe Electrical Discharges on Trees under Thunderstorms
Science.detail: Scientists have observed electrical discharges occurring on trees during thunderstorms, providing new insights into atmospheric electricity.

Science.headline: The climate cost of staying cool: How AC could impact global warming by 2050
Science.detail: The increasing use of air conditioning is projected to significantly impact global warming by 2050, highlighting the need for more sustainable cooling solutions.

Science.headline: PFAS exposure greater in wet pet food, study suggests
Science.detail: A recent study indicates that wet pet food contains higher levels of PFAS, raising concerns about potential health risks to pets.

Science.headline: Missing methane: Countries may be underestimating wastewater greenhouse gas emissions
Science.detail: Research suggests that many countries are underestimating the amount of methane released from wastewater, which could have significant implications for greenhouse gas emission inventories.

Health.headline: HIV can develop resistance to blockbuster antiviral lenacapavir—but at a cost to the virus
Health.detail: Researchers have found that while HIV can develop resistance to the antiviral lenacapavir, it comes at a cost to the virus's overall fitness. This suggests potential strategies for maintaining the drug's effectiveness.

Health.headline: Ketamine reduces anxiety and social withdrawal in stressed adolescent mice
Health.detail: A recent study showed that ketamine can alleviate anxiety and social withdrawal symptoms in adolescent mice exposed to stress. The findings may offer insights into treating similar conditions in humans.

Health.headline: Scientists have built a massive cellular atlas showing how aging reshapes the body across 21 organs
Health.detail: A detailed cellular atlas, encompassing nearly 7 million cells, reveals that aging starts earlier than previously thought and progresses in a coordinated manner throughout the body. This research provides a comprehensive view of how aging affects different organs.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover a Bacterial Kill Switch and It Could Change the Fight Against Superbugs
Health.detail: Researchers have identified that certain viruses can disable a crucial bacterial function, offering a potential new avenue for combating drug-resistant bacteria. This discovery could lead to novel strategies for developing antibiotics.

Health.headline: EMA Backs First Combined mRNA Vaccine for COVID-19 and Flu
Health.detail: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has endorsed the first combined mRNA vaccine targeting both COVID-19 and influenza. This approval marks a significant step in simplifying vaccination strategies for these respiratory illnesses.

Sports.headline: India Women vs Australia Women LIVE Score, 3rd ODI: Harmanpreet Kaur's IND-W seek revenge vs AUS-W in Alyssa Healy's last ODI
Sports.detail: The Indian Women's cricket team is playing against the Australian Women's team in the 3rd ODI, with India looking to avenge their previous loss in Alyssa Healy's final ODI match.

Sports.headline: J&K cricket team scripts history, wins maiden Ranji title
Sports.detail: The Jammu and Kashmir cricket team has achieved a historic milestone by winning their first-ever Ranji title.

Sports.headline: Celtics crank up offense to record levels while awaiting Tatum return
Sports.detail: The Boston Celtics' offense reached peak performance even with Tatum absent, showcasing their depth and offensive capabilities.

Sports.headline: First Lamine Yamal Hat-Trick Helps Liga Leaders Barcelona Beat Villarreal
Sports.detail: Lamine Yamal scored a hat-trick, leading Barcelona to a victory against Villarreal.

Sports.headline: Pakistan crash out of T20 World Cup despite victory against Sri Lanka
Sports.detail: Despite their victory against Sri Lanka, Pakistan was eliminated from the T20 World Cup.

Entertainment.headline: Pink Crashes 'The Kelly Clarkson Show' as She Eyes Time Slot Takeover
Entertainment.detail: Pink made a surprise appearance on 'The Kelly Clarkson Show', playfully suggesting she might take over Clarkson's time slot.

Entertainment.headline: On Set of 'CIA': Tom Ellis and Nick Gehlfuss Team Up for Unexpected Procedural (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: Tom Ellis and Nick Gehlfuss are joining forces in a new procedural series called 'CIA'.

Entertainment.headline: 'Heated Rivalry' Season 2 Gets Production Start Date Ahead of Connor Storrie 'SNL' Debut
Entertainment.detail: Production for the second season of 'Heated Rivalry' is set to begin, coinciding with Connor Storrie's debut on 'SNL'.

Entertainment.headline: Pray for peace to return in Manipur: Boong director after BAFTA win.
Entertainment.detail: Director of 'Boong' film appeals for peace in Manipur after winning a BAFTA award.

Entertainment.headline: CBS News Sunday Morning Listings for March 1, 2026
Entertainment.detail: CBS News Sunday Morning will feature a profile of composer Marc Shaiman, an interview with Jack Schlossberg (grandson of JFK), and a conversation with former Goldman Sachs CEO Lloyd Blankfein.

Crime & Justice.headline: Witness describes chaos during lockdown at Mall of Stonecrest
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting at the Mall of Stonecrest on Saturday left one person injured and led to several detentions as DeKalb police locked down the building.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ex-MLB pitcher Dan Serafini gets life in prison for shooting in-laws
Crime & Justice.detail: Daniel Serafini, who pitched for 11 years in the MLB, will spend the rest of his life in prison for killing his father-in-law and shooting his mother-in-law in 2021.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former imam found guilty of multiple rapes and sexual assaults
Crime & Justice.detail: Abdul Halim Khan, 54, has been found guilty of twenty one counts of rape and sexual assault as well as child sexual offences against seven women.

Crime & Justice.headline: Fake French tourists jailed for smuggling migrants to UK
Crime & Justice.detail: Three men and three women from France who pretended to be tourists were jailed today (Friday 27 February) after they were caught in a convoy of cars bringing three Vietnamese nationals into the UK.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rapist who targeted woman after she left nightclub is sentenced after he blamed family member for the attack
Crime & Justice.detail: A man who raped a woman after she left a Southampton nightclub has today been sentenced for his horrific crimes – after trying to blame his actions on a family member.

Education.headline: Worsening Secondary Teacher Shortage Demands Urgent Action
Education.detail: A recent report shows a deepening shortage of secondary teachers across Aotearoa, leading to cancelled subjects and larger class sizes; education leaders are urging the government to prioritize long-term solutions. The shortage is attributed to teachers leaving for better pay and unfilled vacancies.

Education.headline: Canada, India Boost Education Ties with New Strategy
Education.detail: Canada and India are strengthening their education ties through a new strategy that facilitates research, student exchanges, hybrid campuses, and AI centers of excellence. This agreement aims to drive economic growth and deepen connections between the two countries.

Education.headline: Why real-world problems belong in the classroom
Education.detail: Universities are being encouraged to incorporate problem-based learning (PBL) to equip students with the ability to apply theoretical knowledge to real-world conditions. PBL is an open-ended instructional approach that places students at the centre of the learning process, helping them develop judgement, collaboration and technical reasoning.

Education.headline: 19 Charged by Tablelands Crime Unit in Five Days
Education.detail: Tablelands Crime Unit has charged 19 individuals in five days, although the specific connection to education isn't detailed in the summary. More information is needed to clarify the relevance to education.

Education.headline: 51 Impaired Drivers Caught During Monash Freeway Blitz
Education.detail: Authorities apprehended 51 impaired drivers during a blitz on the Monash Freeway; the context does not explain how this relates to the field of education. Further details are needed to establish any connection to education.

Lifestyle.headline: Madonna is surprise attraction at Dolce & Gabbana Milan show
Lifestyle.detail: Madonna's appearance at the Dolce & Gabbana show in Milan overshadowed the brand's all-black collection, with the singer being the week's top celebrity sighting. The collection heavily featured black lace and sheer silk organza, with lingerie-inspired minidresses, flowing skirts, and see-through tops.

Lifestyle.headline: New American Heart Association report predicts 59% of women will have high blood pressure by 2050
Lifestyle.detail: A new report from the American Heart Association projects that 59% of women will have high blood pressure by 2050, an increase from 49% today, in addition to higher rates of diabetes and obesity. The report highlights a concerning trend in women's cardiovascular health.

Lifestyle.headline: Oakland Zoo celebrates 'milestone' birth of rare red-tailed Guenon monkey
Lifestyle.detail: The Oakland Zoo has announced the birth of a rare red-tailed Guenon monkey, calling it a significant milestone for the zoo and the species. This birth contributes to the conservation efforts for this unique primate.

Lifestyle.headline: My generation was raised to say please and thank you write letters by hand arrive on time and treat every person in the room like they mattered and now we're being told we're out of touch by people who can't make eye contact without a screen in between and I refuse to apologize for the way I was raised
Lifestyle.detail: A recent article discusses how the values of presence, punctuality, and handwritten notes might be exactly what our disconnected world desperately needs. It reflects on the importance of human decency and respect in an age of digital connection.

Lifestyle.headline: A colorful 'flour war' in Greece marks the start of Lent, in photos.
Lifestyle.detail: A colorful 'flour war' in Greece marks the start of Lent. This tradition involves people throwing flour at each other, signaling the beginning of the Lenten season.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Privileged absenteeism
Opinion/Editorial.detail: In any public or private workplace, it’s “no work, no pay,” except if you're a specific senator, highlighting a contrast with a government memorandum reminding employees about work attendance.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: NP View: How the Liberals broke Canada's asylum system
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The government fast-tracked refugees from the most dangerous places on Earth, with very little vetting.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Iran will respond to US-Israeli strikes as existential threats to the regime – because they are
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The latest attack on Iran goes far beyond previous operations by Israel and the US in both scale and scope, suggesting Tehran will likely lean on unconventional warfare and its missile stockpile.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A world on edge as Trump bombs Iran and triggers war in the Middle East
Opinion/Editorial.detail: There was no need for this conflict, and while the end is uncertain, new hatreds will be seeded, terrorist vendettas sown, and ultimately, little will be achieved.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Taking sides: On Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Israel
Opinion/Editorial.detail: India's relations with Israel should not be at the cost of ties with Arab world.

World.headline: IRAN CONFIRMS KHAMENEI'S KILLING IN US-ISRAEL STRIKES
World.detail: Iran has confirmed that Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran, was killed in joint US-Israeli air strikes. The state news agency declared 40 days of mourning.

World.headline: US, Israel bomb Iran: A timeline of talks and threats leading up to attacks
World.detail: The US and Israel have conducted bombing campaigns in Iran following a period of escalating tensions and talks. The strikes mark a significant development in the region.

World.headline: Explosions rock Bahrain, Dubai, Jordan and Kuwait as war spreads
World.detail: Multiple explosions have been reported across Bahrain, Dubai, Jordan, and Kuwait, indicating a widening conflict in the Middle East. The incidents follow US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

World.headline: US backs Pakistan's 'right to defend itself' against Taliban after strikes on Afghanistan
World.detail: The United States has expressed its support for Pakistan's right to defend itself following strikes against Taliban targets in Afghanistan. This comes amid heightened tensions between the two neighboring countries.

World.headline: Syria's central bank launches reform review with top US consultant to rebuild global trust
World.detail: The Syrian central bank has initiated a reform review with a leading US consultant to restore confidence in its financial system. This move is seen as crucial for encouraging foreign banks to re-enter the market.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ governor says still early days to reduce 35% interest rate
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) governor indicated that it is too early to reduce the 35% interest rate. This statement suggests that current monetary policy will likely remain in place for the near future.

Zimbabwe.headline: Scottland FC Jersey Signed by President Mnangagwa Sells for US$1 Million
Zimbabwe.detail: A football jersey signed by the President of Zimbabwe, Emmerson Mnangagwa, was sold for US$1 Million. The jersey was from Scottland FC.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ Drops 2030 Deadline for Multi-Currency Exit, Sets Economic Conditions for ZiG Transition
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has dropped the 2030 deadline for exiting the multi-currency system and has set economic conditions for transitioning to the ZiG. This indicates a shift in the government's approach to currency issues.

Zimbabwe.headline: Information Ministry ramps up Independence Day preparations
Zimbabwe.detail: The Ministry of Information is increasing preparations for Independence Day. No further details were provided.

Zimbabwe.headline: GRANNY CRUSHED TO DEATH: Bedroom collapses on sleeping woman
Zimbabwe.detail: A grandmother was crushed to death when her bedroom collapsed on her while she was sleeping. No further details were provided.
